today_picks.headline: Nick Martinez and Sandy Alcantara, indoors, on a 7.5 — the math is loud.
today_picks.the_thesis:
Nick Martinez is having an absurd run — 1.70 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, 5.2% walk rate across 47.2 innings. The K rate (16.1%) is modest so this isn't a strikeout machine — it's pure pitch-to-contact efficiency with elite command. He doesn't beat himself and Tropicana's an indoor park (no weather, HR factor 0.92) that rewards exactly that profile.

Sandy Alcantara on the other side is more 2026 vintage than vintage Sandy (3.90 ERA, 8.3% BB%) but he's averaging 19 IP per three starts — meaning he goes deep, eats innings, and keeps games tight. The combined HR/9 between these two is 1.20 across 105 innings. That's not how 8-run games happen.

7.5 with both arms regularly going six-plus, in a HR-suppressing dome, against two below-average offenses, at near-coin-flip juice. That's a clean play.
today_picks.the_market: Total opened 7.5 and has stayed 7.5 — books are firm. Under juice cheapest at FD -102, the side we want.
today_picks.weather_park: Indoor at Tropicana — no weather, park HR factor 0.92, runs 0.95. All tailwinds for the under.
today_picks.case_against: Martinez's K rate is low enough (16.1%) that he relies on weak contact — one bad night where balls find grass and you give up four cheap runs. Alcantara has also been hit-or-miss this year despite the innings. And 7.5 is a low number — a single three-run inning per side and it's gone.
today_picks.what_were_betting_on: Both starters into the 7th, neither giving up a crooked number, and the bullpens not needing more than 5 outs combined.
today_picks.scott_bot_quip: Indoors, two grown-up starters, a 7.5 hanging there at even money. We don't need to overthink this one, boys.
today_picks.slate_assessment: Saturday board has three workable angles — two pitching-dominant unders backed by sub-2 ERAs and a wobbly-control Rodón at plus money. Nothing screaming hammer, but real edges if you trust the arms.

today_picks.headline: Rodón is back from elbow surgery and can't find the zone — plus money on the home dog plays.
today_picks.the_thesis:
Carlos Rodón is making his second start since October elbow surgery. The debut was a five-walk, 4.1-inning, 42-of-78-strikes mess against Milwaukee. His season line — 6.23 ERA, 23.8% walk rate, 1.62 WHIP — is from one start so it's noisy, but the underlying signal isn't: he could not throw a strike. When a left-hander with that much fastball can't command, a patient lineup runs his pitch count and you're in the Yankees bullpen by the fifth.

On the other side the Mets are starting Huascar Brazobán, who's been quietly excellent in a hybrid role — 2.14 ERA, 0.95 WHIP, 0.43 HR/9 over 21 innings. Small sample, but you don't need him to throw a no-hitter, you need him to give the Mets four-plus solid innings and let their pen finish. Citi Field is HR-suppressing (park HR factor 0.91), which neuters the Yankee lineup's biggest weapon.

The Mets are home, getting plus money, against a pitcher whose control is in question and whose team can't necessarily protect him with depth (Cole still out per recent reports). At +104 the implied probability is 49% — I have the Mets closer to 53%, which is the 8 cents of edge I need.
today_picks.the_market: Mets opened around +110 and have been bet down to +104 across all three books. Modest move but consistent direction — sharper money likes the Mets here.
today_picks.weather_park: Citi Field, park HR factor 0.91 — neutralizes the Yankee lineup's biggest weapon.
today_picks.case_against: Rodón's debut velocity actually ticked up (95.7 mph vs 94.1 last year) and a second outing back from injury is often where command sharpens. If he settles in, the Yankees lineup (Judge, Bellinger, Chisholm) puts up four off Brazobán and the Mets pen, and we're toast. We're also betting on a 21-inning sample arm to outpitch a real major-league lefty — that's a leap.
today_picks.what_were_betting_on: Rodón walking 4+ guys and not getting through five, Brazobán keeping it manageable, and the Mets pen holding a tight lead at home.
today_picks.scott_bot_quip: When a guy walks five in four innings in start one, start two is rarely the magical fix. Take the plus money and the home park.
today_picks.slate_assessment: Saturday board has three workable angles — two pitching-dominant unders backed by sub-2 ERAs and a wobbly-control Rodón at plus money. Nothing screaming hammer, but real edges if you trust the arms.

today_picks.headline: Two sub-2.00 ERAs on the bump and a Red Sox offense in a full-blown coma.
today_picks.the_thesis:
Bryce Elder is sitting on a 1.81 ERA with a 1.01 WHIP through 54.2 innings — not a flukey month, an actual sustained run. He's not striking out the world (24.4% K%) but he limits the loud contact (0.5 HR/9). Payton Tolle on the other side is the more impressive arm by rate: 1.99 ERA, 0.84 WHIP, 31% K rate through his first 22.2 MLB innings. Yes the sample's smaller, but the stuff is real and the under is 4-for-4 against the spread the boys care about — Boston has failed to cover in each of his four starts because nobody is scoring runs in his games.

The bigger thing pushing this under: Boston's offense is dead. They've gone 1, 2, 1, 3, 1, 2 in their last six games per the Saturday previews. You don't run a power outage like that into Bryce Elder at Truist and suddenly find five runs. And on the Atlanta side, the Braves have managed just 7 total runs across their last three games with Acuña still on the IL. Two cold lineups, two starters with ERAs starting with 1. The math works.

The market opened at 7.88 and has come down to 7.5 — that's a half-run of movement to the under across the industry. That's not retail money doing that; books needed to move that line.
today_picks.the_market: Total opened around 7.9 and has been bet down to a firm 7.5 across all three books. FD -102 is the best number we can get on the under — when juice is that flat on a moved line, the books are confident the number's right.
today_picks.weather_park: Truist Park is mildly hitter-friendly (runs PF 1.02, HR PF 1.04). Not a factor on a 7.5 with these arms.
today_picks.case_against: Elder has reverse splits — righties are actually hitting .268 against him this year and the Red Sox lineup, dead as it is, runs mostly righty. If Elder gives up an early three-spot and Boston's bullpen gets exposed (Atlanta's pen is fine but Boston's has cracks), this gets to 8 in a hurry. Also, both arms have to actually navigate six innings — Tolle averages shorter outings, which puts the under on the back of relievers.
today_picks.what_were_betting_on: Both starters going 5-plus innings of two-run-or-less ball, the Boston cold streak continuing one more night, and neither bullpen melting down.
today_picks.scott_bot_quip: Two ERAs that start with a 1. A Red Sox lineup that's forgotten what a baseball is. We're not getting fancy here, boys.
today_picks.ladder_note: Highest-floor play on the board near even money — two dominant starters, two ice-cold lineups, line already moved our way. This is what a ladder pick looks like.
today_picks.slate_assessment: Saturday board has three workable angles — two pitching-dominant unders backed by sub-2 ERAs and a wobbly-control Rodón at plus money. Nothing screaming hammer, but real edges if you trust the arms.
